title: Conjecture 1 (Trotignon and Vušković)
context_text: Trotignon and Vušković proposed this conjecture in [5] while studying the structure of ISK4-free graphs, proving several special cases. A subsequent paper [2] proved the conjecture with 3 replaced by 4, before the present paper resolves it completely.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full by establishing the stronger structural result (Theorem 2) that every $\{ISK4, \text{triangle}\}$-free graph either has a clique cutset, is complete bipartite, or has a vertex of degree at most two, from which 3-colorability follows easily.
paper_title: Triangle-free graphs that do not contain an induced subdivision of $K_4$ are 3-colorable
